comments.body:
@sepo-agent /fix-pr 

Check the modified readme: 
1. fix any typos in the updated writing and slightly polish it 
2. follow the similar style, add one or two sentences to describe the three parts labeled as TBD

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/update-readme`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Updated [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/update-readme/README.md) with targeted typo fixes and light wording polish in the newly edited sections.
- Replaced all three `[TBD]` placeholders with concise, style-aligned descriptions for:
  - Automatic Task Orchestration Layer
  - Tracking Workspace Memory and Rubrics
  - Scheduled Jobs
- Kept the change surgical to the requested file only.

<details><summary>Validation</summary>

- Confirmed the triggering comment (`issue_comment` 4332675244) was the latest actionable request.
- Checked PR context (`gh pr view`, review comments, and diff): no additional pending review feedback to address.
- Verified README no longer contains placeholder/typo patterns from the request.

</details>

@sepo-agent /fix-pr

good; for scheduled tasks, point to the daily summary as well

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/update-readme`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Addressed the latest actionable request from [comment #4332716721](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3#issuecomment-4332716721).
- Updated the **Scheduled Jobs** paragraph in [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/update-readme/README.md) to explicitly point to daily summaries via `agent-daily-summary.yml`.
- Kept the change surgical: one targeted sentence addition, no unrelated edits.

<details><summary>Validation</summary>

- Fetched trigger comment, PR metadata, PR comments, and current PR diff.
- Confirmed no newer actionable review comments were pending.
- Verified the README now includes the daily summary reference in the scheduled tasks section.

</details>

@sepo-agent /fix-pr 

when referring to the existing content in the readme, be sure to include a link

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/update-readme`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Addressed the latest actionable request from [comment #4332729241](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3#issuecomment-4332729241).
- Updated the **Scheduled Jobs** section in [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/update-readme/README.md) to include a direct link to the referenced existing workflow: [`agent-daily-summary.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/update-readme/.github/workflows/agent-daily-summary.yml).
- Kept the fix surgical: one targeted link addition, no unrelated changes.

<details><summary>Validation</summary>

- Fetched trigger comment, PR metadata, PR comments, and current PR diff.
- Confirmed no newer actionable review comments superseded this request.
- Verified README now links the scheduled-task workflow reference.

</details>

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 69 | PARTIAL | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | partial | 4/8 | PR touches only [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files), but the diff is a broad rewrite (title change, section restructuring, table removals) beyond a narrow “fine-tune/typo + TBD fill” scope. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 6/7 | New references resolve and are actionable (for example, [`agent-daily-summary.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/update-readme/.github/workflows/agent-daily-summary.yml) exists; deployment/access-policy doc links are valid). |
| coding_workflow / generic | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | partial | 4/8 | New “Automatic Task Orchestration Layer” text says automation can chain follow-ups, but does not mention the one-shot default for later user-launched follow-up rounds from this rubric. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | The previous route/label inventory tables were removed and replaced with principle/examples prose in [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files). |

## Notes

- Main score drivers: strong alignment on doc-structure preferences and valid links, offset by scope breadth and missing one-shot orchestration nuance.
- Smallest useful follow-up: tighten the orchestration paragraph to explicitly include the one-shot follow-up default, and (optionally) trim unrelated README rewrites if the intent was strictly surgical.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** The orchestration section in [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files) does not capture the selected rubric’s one-shot follow-up default, so doc guidance is incomplete relative to team preference.
- **WARNING:** Change scope appears broader than a surgical “fine-tune” pass under the selected surgical-changes rubric.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #3 updates `README.md` to polish wording and replace three prior `[TBD]` sections with concrete descriptions for orchestration, memory/rubrics, and scheduled jobs. It also adds a scheduled-jobs link to `agent-daily-summary.yml`, matching follow-up requester feedback. Beyond that requested scope, the PR also rewrites major parts of the “What You Can Ask It To Do” section and quick-start/auth wording. The net result is improved new section content, but with a few behavior-accuracy and guidance regressions in the current diff.

## Review
Overall judgment: the PR is close, but there are still documentation-accuracy gaps that should be fixed before merge. I posted two targeted inline comments for line-specific issues: [auth guardrail](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3#discussion_r3151926106) and [label trigger scope](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3#discussion_r3151926104).

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Quick-start auth guardrail removed | WARNING | The explicit hosted-app/OIDC warning about not setting `AGENT_APP_ID` / `AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` was removed, increasing risk of first-time setup misconfiguration. |
| Label trigger scope is now incorrect | WARNING | README says `agent/*` labels apply to PRs, but workflow behavior supports both issues and PRs. |
| Route reference coverage regressed | WARNING | Replacing the route table with examples removed explicit mention of supported built-ins like `/answer` and `/create-action`, reducing completeness of quick reference docs. |
| Orchestrator section misses one-shot follow-up default nuance | WARNING | The new orchestration text describes chaining/guardrails but omits the selected rubric’s “later user-launched follow-ups default to one-shot” guidance. |

## Progress
- User-requested typo/polish + TBD replacement work has already been completed across prior `/fix-pr` rounds.
- Scheduled jobs now include a direct link to [`agent-daily-summary.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/9385add79e0673e3eb58e88e77b12fcc496e2ee6/.github/workflows/agent-daily-summary.yml), matching request follow-up.
- No existing inline comments covered the two concrete line-level issues above before posting.
- Reviewer disagreement to note: one review treats table removal as acceptable style shift, while another flags loss of command discoverability/completeness.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Quick-start auth guardrail removed</summary>

**Cause:** The Quick Start auth step was collapsed, dropping the explicit hosted-app/OIDC warning text.

**Candidate solutions:**
1. Restore a short dedicated auth-path bullet that explicitly says not to set `AGENT_APP_ID` / `AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` for hosted mode.
2. Keep the new wording but add the same warning sentence inline under installation/auth.

**Comments:** This is actionable on changed line(s) and already posted inline.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Label trigger scope is now incorrect</summary>

**Cause:** README wording says label triggers are for PRs only.

**Candidate solutions:**
1. Change wording to “issues and PRs.”
2. Add one explicit sentence referencing both trigger events for clarity.

**Comments:** Behavior is documented in `agent-label.yml` (`issues.labeled` and `pull_request_target.labeled`).
</details>

<details>
<summary>Route reference coverage regressed</summary>

**Cause:** The previous complete table was replaced by a shorter example block that no longer explicitly lists all built-in routes.

**Candidate solutions:**
1. Reintroduce a concise complete route list/table.
2. Keep examples but add a full built-in route list directly below them.

**Comments:** This is mainly a discoverability/completeness regression, not a runtime behavior change.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Orchestrator section misses one-shot follow-up default nuance</summary>

**Cause:** New text explains chaining and guardrails but omits the selected rubric’s one-shot follow-up default for later user-launched rounds.

**Candidate solutions:**
1. Add one sentence clarifying one-shot default for later follow-up rounds.
2. Link directly to the orchestration behavior doc section that states this policy.

**Comments:** Rubrics review called this out explicitly; adding one sentence is likely sufficient.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: unresolved findings are concrete doc edits on this PR branch and are safe for an automated fix pass.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Restore explicit hosted-app/OIDC guardrail text in Quick Start, including the “do not set `AGENT_APP_ID` / `AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` for hosted mode” warning.
- [ ] Update label-trigger wording to state `agent/*` labels work on both issues and PRs.
- [ ] Add a complete built-in route reference (including `/answer` and `/create-action`) in the “What You Can Ask It To Do” section.
- [ ] Add one sentence in the orchestration subsection clarifying the one-shot default nuance for later user-launched follow-up rounds.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 2/5).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis on PR #3 explicitly recommends `FIX_PR` with unresolved, concrete, low-risk README edits; round 1/5 leaves budget for one automated fix pass.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777356737942 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUwMzY4MTMxMDA6cmV2aWV3OjM6Zml4X3ByOjM6Mg -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/update-readme`. Requested by @lolipopshock.

- Addressed the latest actionable README feedback from the PR’s current review synthesis and inline comments (workflow-dispatch run, no trigger comment ID).
- Updated [README.md](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/agent/update-readme/README.md) to restore the hosted app/OIDC guardrail: explicitly warns not to set `AGENT_APP_ID` / `AGENT_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` for hosted mode.
- Corrected label-trigger wording to match behavior: `agent/*` labels apply to **issues and PRs**.
- Added a concise built-in route reference list, including `/answer` and `/create-action`, alongside the existing examples.
- Added one-shot follow-up nuance to the orchestration section: after the first automatic handoff, later user-launched follow-ups default to one-shot unless explicitly requested.
- Kept changes surgical to `README.md` only.

<details><summary>Validation</summary>

- Fetched PR metadata, review comments, and current diff.
- Confirmed actionable items from current synthesis/inline review were doc-accuracy fixes in README.
- Performed lightweight checks by inspecting the resulting diff and verifying key updated phrases are present.

</details>

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 3/5).

agent planner selected review: Latest action `fix-pr` succeeded on PR #3 and claims to address all previously synthesized WARNING findings; run a verification review to confirm closure before stopping.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777356874933 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUwMzcwMTgyMjk6Zml4X3ByOjM6cmV2aWV3OjM6Mw -->

I’ve got the live PR metadata and full diff; next I’m mapping only the truly applicable rubrics and scoring each with evidence from the current `README.md` changes.## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 25/29 (86) | PARTIAL | 4 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | partial | 4/8 | Only one file changed (`README.md`), but the diff includes broader restructuring (title change, removed mermaid block, rewritten quick-start and command-reference sections) beyond a narrow “fine-tune” scope: <https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files>. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | New behavior text is reflected in docs and references concrete workflow artifacts, including the scheduled daily summary workflow link (`.github/workflows/agent-daily-summary.yml`) in the updated Scheduled Jobs section: <https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files>. |
| coding_workflow / specific | Default follow-up orchestration to one-shot | pass | 8/8 | The added “Automatic Task Orchestration Layer” paragraph explicitly states the one-shot default after first automatic handoff unless explicitly requested. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Prior route/label inventory tables were removed and replaced with principle-oriented prose and concise route bullets, aligning with the rubric’s anti-inventory preference. |

## Notes

- Biggest score driver is scope: content quality improved, but change breadth is larger than a surgical README fine-tune.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed beyond optionally trimming nonessential rewrites if strict surgical scope is required.

## Findings

- **WARNING:** `Make surgical changes` is only partially satisfied because the PR does more than targeted polishing/TBD completion, despite being limited to one file.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PARTIAL

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
PR #3 updates [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files) to polish wording and replace previously `[TBD]` sections with concrete descriptions of orchestration, memory/rubrics, and scheduled jobs. It also reflects prior feedback by restoring auth guardrails, clarifying issue/PR label scope, and linking scheduled jobs to `agent-daily-summary.yml`. I checked the current PR thread and inline comments, and found no referenced parent issue/plan links in the PR metadata that add extra scope constraints. I synthesized all three review artifacts (`claude-review-3`, `codex-review-3`, `rubrics-review-3`) and deduplicated overlapping points. The remaining concern is small and doc-only.

## Review
Overall judgment: content is largely in good shape and prior major doc-accuracy concerns appear resolved, but one line-level formatting issue remains and is worth fixing before merge.  
A targeted inline comment was posted for that specific line: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3#discussion_r3151962659

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Example block uses `python` fence for non-Python content | WARNING | The command/mention example is prose-style GitHub usage, not Python; `python` highlighting is misleading and should be `md` or `text`. |
| Scope breadth vs “surgical changes” rubric | INFO | Reviewers disagree on importance here: one flags broader README restructuring as non-surgical, while others consider the current state acceptable after follow-up fixes. |

## Progress
- Previously raised WARNINGs about auth guardrails and label trigger scope were already addressed in the latest PR state.
- One-shot follow-up nuance and scheduled-jobs linkage were added and now align with prior feedback.
- New inline feedback was posted only for the remaining uncovered line-level issue (code fence language).

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Example block uses `python` fence for non-Python content</summary>

**Cause:** The fenced snippet at README line 31 is tagged `python` even though it demonstrates GitHub comment/route text, not executable Python.

**Candidate solutions:** Change the fence to `md` or `text`, or remove the language tag.

**Comments:** This was raised by one reviewer as WARNING and by another as INFO; either way the fix is low-risk and improves readability.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: there is one unresolved, concrete, low-risk README edit that can be applied directly.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Update the fenced example block language in [`README.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3/files) from `python` to `md` or `text`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/5).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis for PR #3 concludes MINOR_ISSUES and explicitly recommends FIX_PR for one concrete unresolved README edit; round 3/5 leaves budget for an automated fix pass.

- Reviewed PR #3 metadata, full issue/review comment history, inline review comments, and diff: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/3
- Checked trust signals:
  - Repo owner is `self-evolving` (org).
  - No admin/maintain collaborators were returned by collaborator lookup.

- Checked existing active rubrics in `agent/rubrics`; the themes in this PR discussion (surgical scope, docs accuracy, linking referenced content, orchestration nuance) are already covered and/or discussed only by untrusted/bot sources.

Result: no durable trusted new preference to add or update.
